q:
Which of the following reagent is used for the following reaction?

CH3CH2CH3 →?CH3CH2CHO
question:
Which of the following reagent is used for the following reaction?

CH3CH2CH3 →?CH3CH2CHO
options: Copper at high temperature and pressure
solution:
CH3-CH2-CH3 →Mo2O3CH3-CH2-CH=O 

The reagent used will be Mo2O3 (molybdenum oxide) as this reagent always used to oxidise alkanes to aldehyde. So, it is widely used as an oxidation catalyst for acid-mediated catalytic reactions.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the above compound/s does/do not form Silver mirror when treated with Tollen's reagent?
question: Which among the above compound/s does/do not form Silver mirror when treated with Tollen's reagent?
solution:
Aldehyde, alpha-hydroxy ketone and hemiacetals give positive Tollen's test. Enol which can get converted into aldehyde through tautomerism are also give positive Tollen's test. 

(I) is aldehyde, (II) get converted into ketones through tautomerism, (III) get converted into aldehyde through tautomerism and (IV) is hemiacetal so, (II) will not give positive Tollen's test.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Among the following the number of compounds which will give positive iodoform reaction is _____.

(a) 1-Phenylbutan-2-one

(b) 2-Methylbutan-2-ol

(c) 3-Methylbutan-2-ol

(d) 1-Phenylethanol

(e) 3, 3-dimethylbutan-2-one

(f) 1-Phenylpropan-2-ol
question:
Among the following the number of compounds which will give positive iodoform reaction is _____.

(a) 1-Phenylbutan-2-one

(b) 2-Methylbutan-2-ol

(c) 3-Methylbutan-2-ol

(d) 1-Phenylethanol

(e) 3, 3-dimethylbutan-2-one

(f) 1-Phenylpropan-2-ol
solution:
Iodoform test is used to check the presence of carbonyl compounds with the structure R-CO-CH3 or alcohols with the structure R-CH(OH)-CH3 in a given unknown substance. The reaction of iodine, a base and a methyl ketone gives a yellow precipitate along with an “antiseptic” smell.

Hence, (c), (d), (e) and (f) give iodoform reaction

subject: Chemistry
q: Cyclohexylamine when treated with nitrous acid yields (P). On treating (P) with PCC results in (Q). When (Q) is heated with dil. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> we get (R) The final product (R) is:
question: Cyclohexylamine when treated with nitrous acid yields (P). On treating (P) with PCC results in (Q). When (Q) is heated with dil. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> we get (R) The final product (R) is:
solution: Cyclohexylamine when treated with nitrous acid yields cyclohexanol.On treating cyclohexanol with PCC which is a mild oxidising agent and oxidises the alcohols into aldehyde (in case of primary alcohol) or ketones (in case of secondary alcohol) in the presence of dichloromethane results cyclohexanone. When cyclohexanone is heated with dilute NaOH dehydration of aldol takes place by the removal of water molecules we get the final product.

The correct statement for product B is. It is
question:
Consider the following reaction

Propanal+Methanal=→(ii) Δ(iii) NaCN(iv) H3O+(i) dil. NaOHProduct B(C5H8O3)

The correct statement for product B is. It is
options: optically active and adds one mole of bromine
racemic mixture and gives a gas with saturated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution
optically active alcohol and is neutrall
solution:
Propanal and methanal undergo aldol condensation reaction with sodium hydroxide. The α,β-unsaturated compound undergo 1,4-conjugated addition with cyanide ion gives a racemic mixture. The cyanide undergo acidic hydrolysis gives carboxylic acid.

Carboxylic acid will give CO2 gas, with NaHCO3 solution

q:
Given below are the four isomeric compounds $(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{S})$

Identify correct statements from below.
A. $\mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R}$ and S will give precipitate with $2,4-\mathrm{DNP}$.
B. P and Q will give positive Bayer's test.
C. Q and R will give sooty flame.
D. R and S will give yellow precipitate with $\mathrm{I}_{2} / \mathrm{NaOH}$.
E. Q alone will deposit silver with Tollen's reagent Choose the correct option.
question:

A. $\mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R}$ and S will give precipitate with $2,4-\mathrm{DNP}$.
B. P and Q will give positive Bayer's test.
C. Q and R will give sooty flame.
D. R and S will give yellow precipitate with $\mathrm{I}_{2} / \mathrm{NaOH}$.
E. Q alone will deposit silver with Tollen's reagent Choose the correct option.
options: A, B, D and E only
solution:
(A) Q, R, S all three give 2, 4 DNP test as they have Aldehyde/ketone group
(C) Q & R gives sooty flame
(E) Q gives Tollens reagent test

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List - I with List - II.
$\begin{array}{l} \text{List - I} & \text{List - II} \\ \text{Reagents} & \text{Reaction Name (Involving aldehydes)} \\ \text{A. } \mathrm{H}_{2}, \mathrm{Pd}-\mathrm{BaSO}_{4} & \text{I. Etard Reaction} \\ \text{B. } \mathrm{SnCl}_{2}, \mathrm{HCl} & \text{II. Rosenmund Reduction} \\ \text{C. } \mathrm{CrO}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}, \mathrm{CS}_{2} & \text{III. Gatterman - Koch Reaction} \\ \text{D. } \mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{HCl}, \text{ Anhyd. } \mathrm{AlCl}_{3} & \text{IV. Stephen Reaction} \end{array}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Match List - I with List - II.
$\begin{array}{l} \text{List - I} & \text{List - II} \\ \text{Reagents} & \text{Reaction Name (Involving aldehydes)} \\ \text{A. } \mathrm{H}_{2}, \mathrm{Pd}-\mathrm{BaSO}_{4} & \text{I. Etard Reaction} \\ \text{B. } \mathrm{SnCl}_{2}, \mathrm{HCl} & \text{II. Rosenmund Reduction} \\ \text{C. } \mathrm{CrO}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}, \mathrm{CS}_{2} & \text{III. Gatterman - Koch Reaction} \\ \text{D. } \mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{HCl}, \text{ Anhyd. } \mathrm{AlCl}_{3} & \text{IV. Stephen Reaction} \end{array}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Matching reagents with aldehyde reaction types:

A (H2, Pd-BaSO4) is the Lindlar/Rosenmund catalyst used in Rosenmund Reduction to convert acyl chlorides to aldehydes, matching with II.

B (SnCl2, HCl) is used in Stephen Reaction where nitriles are converted to aldehydes via imine intermediate, matching with IV.

C (CrO2Cl2, CS2) is Jones-like reagent used in Etard Reaction for oxidizing methyl groups on aromatic rings to aldehydes, matching with I.

D (CO, HCl, Anhyd. AlCl3) is the Gatterman-Koch Reaction which directly converts benzene to benzaldehyde using CO and AlCl3 catalyst, matching with III.

Therefore the correct matching is A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III

q: The compound formed by the reaction of ethanal with semicarbazide contains _______ number of nitrogen atoms.
question: The compound formed by the reaction of ethanal with semicarbazide contains _______ number of nitrogen atoms.
solution:
Semicarbazide is the chemical compound with the formula OC(NH2)(N2H3). It is a water-soluble white solid. It is a derivative of urea.

Acetaldehyde reacts with semicarbazide give acetaldehyde semicarbazone, the reaction will be

Hence the product formed has three nitrogen atoms in it.

question:
. Match List I with List II :

List I (Reagents used) List II (Compound with Functional group detected)
A. Alkaline solution of copper sulphate and sodium citrate I.
B. Neutral FeCl3 solution II.
C. Alkaline chloroform solution III.
D. Potassium iodide and sodium hypochloride IV

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
(A) Aldehydes give red precipitate with Fehling's solution.

(B) Phenols give violet colour with neutral ferric chloride solution

(C) Primary amines give isocyanides with chloroform in the presence of alkali. This reaction known as carbylamine reaction.

(D) 1-phenylethanol give haloform reaction with sodium hypochlorite. The products are benzoate and chloroform.

subject: Chemistry
q:
In the following reaction
carbonyl compound +MeOH⇌HClacetal

Rate of the reaction is the highest for:
question:
In the following reaction
carbonyl compound +MeOH⇌HClacetal

Rate of the reaction is the highest for:
options: Acetone as substrate and methanol in stoichiometric amount
Propanal as substrate and methanol in excess
Propanal as substrate and methanol in stoichiometric amount
Acetone as substrate and methanol in excess
solution:
Acetal is form from the reaction of aldehyde with excess of MeOH

Formation of an acetal occurs when the hydroxyl group of a hemiacetal becomes protonated and is lost as water. The carbocation that is produced is then rapidly attacked by a molecule of alcohol. Loss of the proton from the attached alcohol gives the acetal.

subject: Chemistry
q: m-chlorobenzaldehyde on treatment with 50%KOH solution yields
question: m-chlorobenzaldehyde on treatment with 50%KOH solution yields
solution:
Meta–chlorobenzaldehyde will undergo Cannizzaro reaction with 50% KOH to give mchlorobenzoate ion and m-chlorobenzyl alcohol. 

Since m-chlorobenzaldehyde does not contain α hydrogen thus with 50% KOH it undergoes the Cannizzaro reaction. The Cannizzaro reaction for the m-chlorobenzaldehyde is given as:

Since it is a disproportionation reaction so one molecule of m-chlorobenzaldehyde is reduced to m-chlorobenzyl alcohol at the cost of others which is oxidized potassium m-chlorobenzoate.
